---
title: "Annual CMP application"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.html >}}

<div class="paper-title">Controlled Annual Application Workflow</div>

This workflow describes how an adopted CMP should be applied as new data become
available. Candidate dry runs should follow the same controls.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A["Freeze CMP version and code commit"] --> B["Receive and archive annual data"]
  B --> C["Validate schema, units, completeness, and vintage"]
  C -->|fail| X["Stop and document exception"]
  C -->|pass| D["Calculate estimator and status metric"]
  D --> E["Apply HCR"]
  E --> F["Apply interannual and implementation constraints"]
  F --> G["Run independent or automated cross-check"]
  G -->|mismatch| X
  G -->|match| H["Review exceptional-circumstances indicators"]
  H --> I["Publish advice and complete calculation record"]
```

## 1. Freeze the governing specification

Record the CMP identifier and version, repository commit, software environment,
management year, analyst, reviewer, and decision record authorizing use.

## 2. Archive inputs

Store the received files unchanged. Record provider, receipt time, data vintage,
and checksums. Create a separate validated copy only through a scripted,
reviewable transformation.

## 3. Validate before calculation

Apply the [input data contract](input-data.qmd). A failed required check stops
the routine calculation. The failure and any subsequent authorized treatment
become part of the permanent annual record.

## 4. Calculate transparent intermediates

Save the transformed index values, reference-period values, combined metric,
raw HCR recommendation, each constraint adjustment, allocation, rounding step,
and final advice. Do not save only the final number.

## 5. Cross-check

Compare against a frozen worked example and, where feasible, an independent
implementation. Differences outside the specification’s numerical tolerance
must be resolved before advice is released.

## 6. Exceptional circumstances

Run only the indicators and response process defined in the adopted
specification. An exceptional-circumstances review may trigger additional
scientific or management consideration; it must not silently rewrite the CMP.

## 7. Publish the calculation record

The record should contain inputs and checksums, validation results, intermediate
calculations, final advice, software versions, warnings, analyst and reviewer
sign-off, and links to any exception decision.
